task_completion.description: The WooCommerce Product Import tool is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the WordPress admin dashboard with the 'Import Products' screen open.

task_completion.description: The correct CSV file is selected for upload.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing 'craft_products_import.csv' selected in the file upload input of the WooCommerce importer.

task_completion.description: CSV columns are mapped to WooCommerce product fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of the 'Map CSV fields to products' step in WooCommerce, showing mappings for Name, SKU, Regular Price, and Categories.
A screenshot showing a WooCommerce success message indicating that 8 products were imported.

task_completion.description: The imported products are correctly listed in WooCommerce.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of the WooCommerce Products page showing the newly imported products with matching names, SKUs, prices, and categories as specified in the privileged info.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the WordPress GUI to perform the import.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the web browser navigating the WordPress admin interface, rather than terminal commands (like WP-CLI) being used to import products.

integrity.description: The agent used the bulk import tool rather than creating products manually.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the WooCommerce CSV import workflow being used, rather than the 'Add New Product' screen being used repeatedly to manually enter the 8 products.

integrity.description: The results are displayed in the actual WordPress application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final products list is shown within the standard WordPress admin dashboard UI, not in a text editor or a modified local HTML file.
